content_en:
him if he has sufficient money according to Shafi*! and Ahmed.

According to Imam Malik, physical ability makes Haj obligatory on a Muslim. According to Abu Hanifah, both physical and financial abilities are required for Hajj. Ahmed shares this point of view. None of the Muslim Imams ever said that a very sick person is allowed to perform Hajj or ~Umrah. How comes that he keeps the state of Ihram until he dies and_ is entitled to complete Hajj?

Second: Such woman may return home and then travel to Mecca once again but she may be exposed to the very same conditions of the first journey. She can not return home except with her company and she may menstruate during her stay at Mecca.

Third: This means that such woman will travel twice for Hajj without doing anything illegal. This ruling is not in conformity with the fundamentals of religion. Allah made Hajj obligatory on people just once during their lifetime. A person may perform Hajj again because he committed an illegal act that invalidated his Ihram.

Yet, such person does so because he did not conform with the regular procedures of Hajj such as not standing at ~Arafah. He may have no idea about the road, or followed another which is not tracked by most people, and hence he did not perform Hajj and he should perform it again. A menstruating woman has not made anything in

disconformity with the rules of Hajj. Therefore, the Prophet (peace be upon him) allowed her not to perform the Farewell and Arrival circumambulation as in the traditions narrated by ~ A’ishah and Safiah.

As for the second option that she should put an end to the state of Ihram just like the people prevented from completing Hajj or ~Umrah, this is a more proper argument as said by some scholars. Her fear to stay in Mecca until she circumambulates is similar to her fear of an enemy who prevents her from circumambulation. A Muslim is never enjoined to perform Hajj while there are conditions that may prevent him from doing it. If a person believes that if he intends to perform Hajj, but he will be prevented from completing it, Hajj is not obligatory on him at all. Safe roads to Mecca and enough time to travel are obligatory conditions according to the unanimous agreement of Muslims.

There is dispute whether these conditions necessitate Hajj i.e. when a man has enough money and_ the means of transport but he fears that the way to Mecca may not be safe or there is not enough time to go there, is Hajj obligatory on him? Can anyone perform Hajj on behalf of him after he dies? Or is it not obligatory on him at all? There are two opinions in this regard.

If a scholar says that such woman is prevented trom completing Hajj and hence she can end the state of Ihram, he is entitled to adopt the first option that Hajj is not obligatory or even desirable for women. According to such

argument, Hajj is not legal for most women during these times because they are not able to perform some obligatory acts during circumambulation.

It is well know that this later opinion does not conform with the fundamentals of religion. When one is unable to perform some of the obligatory or desirable acts of worship, he should perform the acts he is able to do. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: “If I commanded you to do something, do whatever you can (of it).” Allah also said: “So fear Allah as much as you can.”

It is also well known that prayer and other acts of worship greater than circumambulation are obligatory even if one can not perform some of its conditions and prerequisites. How comes that Hajj is not obligatory once a person is unable to perform some of its conditions and prerequisites?

It is more suitable to say that it is permissible for her to perform the Ifadah circumambulation before standing at ~Arafah. In this case, it is permissible for her to

circumambulate before or after standing at ~ Arafah. Yet, none of the Muslim Imams has permitted such act. There is an exception reported by the people of Basra who narrated that Imam Malik sees that it is permissible to circumambulate before standing at ~Arafah if one has done so forgetfully or out of ignorance and later returned home. In this case, he is not entitled to perform the Ifadah circumambulation,

Therefore, it may be said that the same case is permissible for a menstruating woman if she has not managed to circumambulate before standing at ~Arafah. Yet, | know none of the Imams who professed such opinion.

As for the case reported by Malik, one may say that when a person forgets to circumambulate or even does not know that it is obligatory, his return to perform Hajj against is a great burden. Therefore, the order is not required for such excuse. The same thing is applicable as far as ritual purity is concerned according to Ahmed’s juristic school. Ifa person circumambulated while he is in a state of ritual impurity, but he forgot so, he has an excuse and therefore, he may slaughter an animal.

If he managed to perform most obligatory acts, how comes that Hajj is not due while he has not done just some acts? It should be known that a menstruating woman’ circumambulation is permissible and she should slaughte: an animal.

As for performing the obligatory circumambulation first before standing at ~Arafah, it is not permissible at all. If one forgets, the order of performing acts of worship is not necessary according to the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars. Yet, if one is unable, the order is necessary as in the prerequisites of prayer. The same thing applies when one has no enough time to perform such acts according to the agreement of the majority of Muslim scholars.

If a post-menstruating woman has the ability to circumambulate before standing at ~Arafah when she is ritually pure, and after standing at ~ Arafah but when she ritually impure, she should not circumambulate except after standing at ~Arafah. From this very perspective, it is not allowed for a woman to fast before the advent of Ramadan because she menstruates at Ramadan. Yet, she fasts once its time is obligatory.

According to the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars, if there is contradiction between the time of a certain act of worship and some of its conditions and prerequisites, ignoring some of the latter is more acceptable. If a person has the ability to pray before time is due with complete ritual purity and to avoid all sorts of impurities, while he can not do the same when its time is due, it is not permissible for him to perform it before its due time according to the Qur’an, Sunnah and the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars. He should perform it at its due time.

Similarly acts of worship are not to be delayed after their

time is due. Instead, they should be done on time as far as possible. Yet, if one has an excuse, it is permissible for him to perform tow prayers together. There are tow appointed times for prayer; one for ordinary people and the other is for those who have an excuse. When a person performs two prayers together at the same time, he performed them at their due legal time. He has not missed any of them neither he did any before its time is due according to the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars.

The same rule is applicable to standing at ~ Arafah. If one stood before or after its time is due, it is not permissible according to the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars. Ifadah circumambulation is to be done after standing at ~Arafah on the Day of Slaughter and the following days. Is it permissible to circumambulate after midnight on the night of Slaughter? This is a controversial point among scholars.

Once these four cases are invalid, there remains the fifth:

She should do what she can and let aside what she can not. This opinion is supported by the divine texts which handle this matter and it is not contradictory with any of the fundamentals of religion.

Divine texts indicate that ritual purity is obligatory as the Prophet (peace be upon him) said: “A menstruating woman is to perform all rituals except for circumambulating around the Sacred House.” This indicates that the command here is obligatory as he said: “When any of you passes urine, feces or the like, he should not perform prayers unless he performed ablution.”

He also said: “Allah will not accept your prayer unless you performed ablution.” He also said: “Allah will not accept the prayer of a major woman unless she wore a veil.” He also advised a woman saying: “Clean it (your dress) well, wash it and then pray while wearing it.” He also said: “A naked person should not circumambulate around the Sacred House.” All these are obligatory on the condition that one is able to perform them as Allah said: “So fear Allah as much as you can.” The Prophet (peace be upon him) also said: “If I commanded you to do something, do whatever you can (of it).”

Now it turns out that she can not stay at Mecca while she is unable because she harbors fears for herself, her religion and money. She is not to be ordered to continue in her state of Ihram, return home and travel to Mecca again although she does not have the ability to do so and the same thing may happen again without the least sin on her part. Putting an end to the state of Ihram is not permissible and the obligatory act of circumambulation is still to be done. Circumambulation is almost similar to prayer.

There is no proof that circumambulation is not accepted if performed by a person ina state of major ritual impurity. Yet, there are proofs that ritual purity is obligatory.

Hence, there is disagreement among scholars whether ritual purity is a condition or just an obligatory act. Such disagreement did not occur as far as prayer is concerned.

The second proof is that ritual purity is a condition for circumambulation. It is well known that ritual purity is
content_id:
dia jika ia memiliki cukup uang menurut Syafi'i dan Ahmad.

Menurut Imam Malik, kemampuan fisik menjadikan Haji wajib bagi seorang Muslim. Menurut Abu Hanifah, baik kemampuan fisik maupun finansial diperlukan untuk Haji. Ahmad berpendapat demikian pula. Tidak ada satu pun dari para Imam Muslim yang pernah mengatakan bahwa orang yang sangat sakit diperbolehkan melaksanakan Haji atau 'Umrah. Bagaimana bisa ia tetap dalam keadaan ihram hingga meninggal dan berhak menyelesaikan Haji?

Kedua: Wanita tersebut dapat pulang ke rumah lalu kembali bepergian ke Makkah, namun ia mungkin menghadapi kondisi yang sama seperti perjalanan pertama. Ia tidak dapat pulang ke rumah kecuali bersama rombongannya, dan ia mungkin mengalami haid selama tinggal di Makkah.

Ketiga: Ini berarti wanita tersebut akan bepergian dua kali untuk Haji tanpa melakukan sesuatu yang melanggar aturan. Ketentuan ini tidak sesuai dengan dasar-dasar agama. Allah mewajibkan Haji atas manusia hanya sekali seumur hidup. Seseorang mungkin melaksanakan Haji kembali karena ia melakukan pelanggaran yang membatalkan ihramnya.

Namun demikian, orang tersebut melakukannya karena ia tidak mematuhi prosedur Haji yang lazim, seperti tidak wukuf di 'Arafah. Ia mungkin tidak mengetahui jalannya, atau mengikuti jalan lain yang tidak ditempuh kebanyakan orang, sehingga ia tidak melaksanakan Haji dan harus mengulanginya. Wanita yang haid tidak melakukan sesuatu pun yang

bertentangan dengan aturan Haji. Oleh karena itu, Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) mengizinkannya untuk tidak melakukan thawaf Wada' dan thawaf Qudum sebagaimana dalam riwayat dari 'A'isyah dan Shafiyyah.

Adapun pilihan kedua bahwa ia harus mengakhiri keadaan ihram sebagaimana orang-orang yang terhalang dari menyelesaikan Haji atau 'Umrah, ini merupakan argumen yang lebih tepat sebagaimana dikatakan oleh sebagian ulama. Kekhawatirannya untuk tinggal di Makkah hingga ia dapat thawaf serupa dengan kekhawatirannya terhadap musuh yang menghalanginya dari thawaf. Seorang Muslim tidak pernah diperintahkan untuk melaksanakan Haji sementara ada kondisi yang mungkin menghalanginya. Jika seseorang meyakini bahwa jika ia berniat melaksanakan Haji, namun ia akan terhalang dari menyelesaikannya, maka Haji sama sekali tidak wajib atasnya. Jalan yang aman menuju Makkah dan waktu yang cukup untuk bepergian merupakan syarat wajib berdasarkan kesepakatan kaum Muslimin.

Terdapat perbedaan pendapat apakah syarat-syarat tersebut mewajibkan Haji, yaitu ketika seseorang memiliki cukup uang dan sarana transportasi namun ia khawatir jalan menuju Makkah tidak aman atau tidak ada cukup waktu untuk pergi ke sana, apakah Haji wajib atasnya? Dapatkah seseorang melaksanakan Haji atas namanya setelah ia meninggal? Ataukah Haji sama sekali tidak wajib atasnya? Terdapat dua pendapat dalam hal ini.

Jika seorang ulama berpendapat bahwa wanita tersebut terhalang dari menyelesaikan Haji sehingga ia dapat mengakhiri keadaan ihram, ia berhak untuk mengambil pilihan pertama bahwa Haji tidak wajib atau bahkan tidak dianjurkan bagi wanita. Menurut

argumen tersebut, Haji tidak sah bagi kebanyakan wanita pada zaman ini karena mereka tidak mampu melaksanakan beberapa amalan wajib selama thawaf.

Telah diketahui bahwa pendapat terakhir ini tidak sesuai dengan dasar-dasar agama. Ketika seseorang tidak mampu melaksanakan sebagian amalan wajib atau amalan yang dianjurkan dalam ibadah, ia hendaknya melaksanakan amalan yang mampu ia lakukan. Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda: "Jika aku memerintahkan kalian untuk melakukan sesuatu, lakukanlah semampunya." Allah juga berfirman: "Maka bertakwalah kepada Allah semampu kalian."

Telah diketahui pula bahwa salah dan amalan ibadah lain yang lebih besar dari thawaf tetap wajib meskipun seseorang tidak dapat memenuhi sebagian syarat dan prasyaratnya. Bagaimana bisa Haji tidak wajib hanya karena seseorang tidak mampu memenuhi sebagian syarat dan prasyaratnya?

Lebih tepat untuk dikatakan bahwa diperbolehkan baginya untuk melaksanakan thawaf Ifadhah sebelum wukuf di 'Arafah. Dalam hal ini, diperbolehkan baginya untuk thawaf sebelum atau sesudah wukuf di 'Arafah. Namun demikian, tidak ada satu pun dari para Imam Muslim yang memperbolehkan tindakan tersebut. Ada pengecualian yang dilaporkan oleh penduduk Basra yang meriwayatkan bahwa Imam Malik berpandangan boleh thawaf sebelum wukuf di 'Arafah jika hal itu dilakukan karena lupa atau ketidaktahuan dan ia kemudian pulang ke rumah. Dalam hal ini, ia tidak berhak melaksanakan thawaf Ifadhah.

Oleh karena itu, dapat dikatakan bahwa kasus yang sama diperbolehkan bagi wanita yang haid jika ia tidak sempat thawaf sebelum wukuf di 'Arafah. Namun, saya tidak mengetahui satu pun dari para Imam yang berpendapat demikian.

Adapun kasus yang dilaporkan oleh Malik, seseorang dapat mengatakan bahwa ketika seseorang lupa thawaf atau bahkan tidak mengetahui bahwa hal itu wajib, kepulangannya untuk melaksanakan Haji kembali merupakan beban yang berat. Oleh karena itu, urutan tersebut tidak diperlukan karena alasan semacam ini. Hal yang sama berlaku sejauh menyangkut thaharah menurut mazhab fiqh Ahmad. Jika seseorang thawaf dalam keadaan hadats, namun ia lupa, ia memiliki uzur dan oleh karena itu ia boleh menyembelih hewan.

Jika ia berhasil melaksanakan sebagian besar amalan wajib, bagaimana bisa Haji tidak sah sementara ia hanya meninggalkan sebagian amalan? Perlu diketahui bahwa thawaf wanita yang haid adalah sah dan ia wajib menyembelih hewan.

Adapun melaksanakan thawaf wajib terlebih dahulu sebelum wukuf di 'Arafah, hal itu sama sekali tidak diperbolehkan. Jika seseorang lupa, urutan pelaksanaan amalan ibadah tidak diperlukan berdasarkan kesepakatan ulama Muslim. Namun, jika seseorang tidak mampu, urutan tersebut diperlukan sebagaimana syarat-syarat salah. Hal yang sama berlaku ketika seseorang tidak memiliki cukup waktu untuk melaksanakan amalan tersebut berdasarkan kesepakatan mayoritas ulama Muslim.

Jika seorang wanita yang telah suci dari haid mampu thawaf sebelum wukuf di 'Arafah dalam keadaan suci, dan setelah wukuf di 'Arafah namun dalam keadaan tidak suci, ia tidak boleh thawaf kecuali setelah wukuf di 'Arafah. Dari sudut pandang inilah, tidak diperbolehkan bagi seorang wanita untuk berpuasa sebelum datangnya Ramadan karena ia haid di Ramadan. Namun, ia berpuasa begitu waktunya telah menjadi wajib.

Berdasarkan kesepakatan ulama Muslim, jika terdapat pertentangan antara waktu suatu amalan ibadah dengan sebagian syarat dan prasyaratnya, mengabaikan sebagian yang terakhir lebih dapat diterima. Jika seseorang mampu salah sebelum waktunya tiba dengan thaharah yang sempurna dan menghindari segala macam najis, sementara ia tidak dapat melakukan hal yang sama ketika waktunya tiba, ia tidak diperbolehkan melaksanakannya sebelum waktunya berdasarkan Al-Qur'an, Sunnah dan kesepakatan ulama Muslim. Ia harus melaksanakannya pada waktunya.

Demikian pula amalan ibadah tidak boleh ditunda setelah

waktunya tiba. Sebaliknya, amalan tersebut harus dilakukan tepat waktu semampu mungkin. Namun, jika seseorang memiliki uzur, diperbolehkan baginya untuk menjama' dua salah. Terdapat dua waktu yang telah ditetapkan untuk salah; satu untuk orang biasa dan yang lainnya untuk mereka yang memiliki uzur. Ketika seseorang menjama' dua salah pada waktu yang sama, ia telah melaksanakannya pada waktu yang sah secara syar'i. Ia tidak melewatkan satu pun dari keduanya maupun melaksanakan salah satunya sebelum waktunya tiba berdasarkan kesepakatan ulama Muslim.

Aturan yang sama berlaku untuk wukuf di 'Arafah. Jika seseorang wukuf sebelum atau sesudah waktunya tiba, hal itu tidak diperbolehkan berdasarkan kesepakatan ulama Muslim. Thawaf Ifadhah dilakukan setelah wukuf di 'Arafah pada Hari Penyembelihan dan hari-hari berikutnya. Apakah diperbolehkan thawaf setelah tengah malam pada malam Penyembelihan? Ini merupakan poin yang diperdebatkan di kalangan ulama.

Setelah keempat kasus tersebut dinyatakan tidak valid, tersisa yang kelima:

Ia hendaknya melakukan apa yang mampu dan meninggalkan apa yang tidak mampu. Pendapat ini didukung oleh nash-nash syar'i yang membahas masalah ini dan tidak bertentangan dengan satu pun dasar-dasar agama.

Nash-nash syar'i menunjukkan bahwa thaharah itu wajib sebagaimana sabda Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam): "Wanita yang haid melaksanakan semua manasik kecuali thawaf di Baitullah." Ini menunjukkan bahwa perintah di sini bersifat wajib sebagaimana beliau bersabda: "Apabila salah seorang dari kalian buang air kecil, buang air besar atau semisalnya, ia tidak boleh salah kecuali telah berwudhu."

Beliau juga bersabda: "Allah tidak akan menerima salatmu kecuali engkau berwudhu." Beliau juga bersabda: "Allah tidak akan menerima salah wanita yang telah baligh kecuali ia mengenakan cadar." Beliau juga menasihati seorang wanita dengan bersabda: "Bersihkan pakaianmu dengan baik, cucilah, kemudian salalah sambil memakainya." Beliau juga bersabda: "Orang yang telanjang tidak boleh thawaf di Baitullah." Semua ini wajib dengan syarat seseorang mampu melaksanakannya sebagaimana firman Allah: "Maka bertakwalah kepada Allah semampu kalian." Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) juga bersabda: "Jika aku memerintahkan kalian untuk melakukan sesuatu, lakukanlah semampunya."

Ternyata ia tidak dapat tinggal di Makkah sementara ia tidak mampu karena ia merasa khawatir terhadap dirinya, agama, dan hartanya. Ia tidak diperintahkan untuk tetap dalam keadaan ihram, pulang ke rumah, dan bepergian ke Makkah lagi meskipun ia tidak memiliki kemampuan untuk itu dan hal yang sama mungkin terjadi lagi tanpa dosa sedikit pun darinya. Mengakhiri keadaan ihram tidak diperbolehkan dan kewajiban thawaf masih harus dilaksanakan. Thawaf hampir serupa dengan salah.

Tidak ada dalil bahwa thawaf tidak diterima jika dilakukan oleh seseorang dalam keadaan hadats besar. Namun, terdapat dalil-dalil bahwa bersuci adalah wajib.

Oleh karena itu, terdapat perbedaan pendapat di antara para ulama apakah bersuci merupakan syarat atau sekadar kewajiban. Perbedaan pendapat seperti itu tidak terjadi dalam hal salah.

Dalil kedua adalah bahwa bersuci merupakan syarat untuk thawaf. Sudah diketahui bahwa bersuci adalah
content_ar: him إذا he has sufficient المال وفقاً لـ Shafi*! and Ahmed. وفقاً لـ الإمام مالك, physical ability makes Haj obligatory on a مسلم. وفقاً لـ Abu Hanifah, both physical and financial abilities are required for الحج. Ahmed shares this point of view. None of the مسلم Imams ever said that a very sick person is allowed to perform الحج or ~العمرة. How comes that he keeps the state of Ihram حتى he dies and_ is entitled to complete الحج? ثانياً: Such woman may return home and then travel to مكة المكرمة حين again but she may be exposed to the very same conditions of the أولاً journey. She can not return home except with her company and she may menstruate خلال her stay at مكة المكرمة. ثالثاً: This means that such woman will travel twice for الحج without doing anything illegal. This ruling is not in conformity with the fundamentals of religion. الله made الحج obligatory on people just حين خلال their lifetime. A person may perform الحج again لأن he committed an illegal act that invalidated his Ihram. Yet, such person does لذا لأن he did not conform with the regular procedures of الحج مثل not standing at ~Arafah. He may have no idea about the road, or followed another which is not tracked by most people, and ومن ثَم he did not perform الحج and he should perform it again. A menstruating woman has not made anything in disconformity with the rules of الحج. لذلك, النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) allowed her not to perform the Farewell and Arrival circumambulation as in the traditions narrated by ~ A’ishah and Safiah. أما the ثانياً option that she should put an end to the state of Ihram just like the people prevented from completing الحج or ~العمرة, this is a more proper argument as said by some العلماء. Her الخوف to stay in مكة المكرمة حتى she circumambulates is similar to her الخوف of an enemy who prevents her from circumambulation. A مسلم is never enjoined to perform الحج بينما there are conditions that may prevent him from doing it. إذا a person believes that إذا he intends to perform الحج, but he will be prevented from completing it, الحج is not obligatory on him at all. Safe roads to مكة المكرمة and enough time to travel are obligatory conditions وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of المسلمون. There is dispute whether these conditions necessitate الحج i.e. عندما a man has enough المال and_ the means of transport but he fears that the الطريق to مكة المكرمة may not be safe or there is not enough time to go there, is الحج obligatory on him? Can anyone perform الحج on behalf of him بعد he dies? Or is it not obligatory on him at all? There are two opinions in this regard. إذا a scholar says that such woman is prevented trom completing الحج and ومن ثَم she can end the state of Ihram, he is entitled to adopt the أولاً option that الحج is not obligatory or even desirable for women. وفقاً لـ such argument, الحج is not legal for most women خلال these times لأن they are not able to perform some obligatory acts خلال circumambulation. It is well know that this later opinion does not conform with the fundamentals of religion. عندما one is unable to perform some of the obligatory or desirable acts of worship, he should perform the acts he is able to do. النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) said: “إذا I commanded you to do something, do whatever you can (of it).” الله أيضاً said: “لذا الخوف الله as much as you can.” It is أيضاً well known that الصلاة and other acts of worship greater than circumambulation are obligatory even إذا one can not perform some of its conditions and prerequisites. How comes that الحج is not obligatory حين a person is unable to perform some of its conditions and prerequisites? It is more suitable to say that يجوز for her to perform the Ifadah circumambulation قبل standing at ~Arafah. In this case, يجوز for her to circumambulate قبل or بعد standing at ~ Arafah. Yet, none of the مسلم Imams has permitted such act. There is an exception reported by the people of Basra who narrated that الإمام مالك sees that يجوز to circumambulate قبل standing at ~Arafah إذا one has done لذا forgetfully or out of ignorance and later returned home. In this case, he is not entitled to perform the Ifadah circumambulation, لذلك, it may be said that the same case is permissible for a menstruating woman إذا she has not managed to circumambulate قبل standing at ~Arafah. Yet, | know none of the Imams who professed such opinion. أما the case reported by مالك, one may say that عندما a person forgets to circumambulate or even does not know that يجب, his return to perform الحج against is a great burden. لذلك, the order is not required for such excuse. The same thing is applicable as far as ritual purity is concerned وفقاً لـ Ahmed’s juristic school. Ifa person circumambulated بينما he is in a state of ritual impurity, but he forgot لذا, he has an excuse and لذلك, he may slaughter an animal. إذا he managed to perform most obligatory acts, how comes that الحج is not due بينما he has not done just some acts? It should be known that a menstruating woman’ circumambulation is permissible and she should slaughte: an animal. أما performing the obligatory circumambulation أولاً قبل standing at ~Arafah, لا يجوز at all. إذا one forgets, the order of performing acts of worship is not necessary وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء. Yet, إذا one is unable, the order is necessary as in the prerequisites of الصلاة. The same thing applies عندما one has no enough time to perform such acts وفقاً لـ the agreement of the majority of مسلم العلماء. إذا a post-menstruating woman has the ability to circumambulate قبل standing at ~Arafah عندما she is ritually pure, and بعد standing at ~ Arafah but عندما she ritually impure, she should not circumambulate except بعد standing at ~Arafah. From this very perspective, it is not allowed for a woman to fast قبل the advent of Ramadan لأن she menstruates at Ramadan. Yet, she fasts حين its time is obligatory. وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء, إذا there is contradiction between the time of a certain act of worship and some of its conditions and prerequisites, ignoring some of the latter is more acceptable. إذا a person has the ability to pray قبل time is due with complete ritual purity and to avoid all sorts of impurities, بينما he can not do the same عندما its time is due, لا يجوز for him to perform it قبل its due time وفقاً لـ the Qur’an, السنة and the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء. He should perform it at its due time. Similarly acts of worship are not to be delayed بعد their time is due. Instead, they should be done on time as far as possible. Yet, إذا one has an excuse, يجوز for him to perform tow الصلوات together. There are tow appointed times for الصلاة; one for ordinary people and the other is for those who have an excuse. عندما a person performs two الصلوات together at the same time, he performed them at their due legal time. He has not missed any of them neither he did any قبل its time is due وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء. The same rule is applicable to standing at ~ Arafah. إذا one stood قبل or بعد its time is due, لا يجوز وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء. Ifadah circumambulation is to be done بعد standing at ~Arafah on the Day of Slaughter and the following days. Is it permissible to circumambulate بعد midnight on the night of Slaughter? This is a controversial point among العلماء. حين these four cases are invalid, there remains the خامساً: She should do what she can and let aside what she can not. This opinion is supported by the divine texts which handle this matter and it is not contradictory with any of the fundamentals of religion. Divine texts indicate that ritual purity is obligatory as النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) said: “A menstruating woman is to perform all rituals except for circumambulating around the Sacred House.” This indicates that the command here is obligatory as قال: “عندما any of you passes urine, feces or the like, he should not perform الصلوات إلا إذا he performed ablution.” He أيضاً said: “الله will not accept your الصلاة إلا إذا you performed ablution.” He أيضاً said: “الله will not accept the الصلاة of a major woman إلا إذا she wore a veil.” He أيضاً advised a woman saying: “Clean it (your dress) well, wash it and then pray بينما wearing it.” He أيضاً said: “A naked person should not circumambulate around the Sacred House.” All these are obligatory on the condition that one is able to perform them as الله said: “لذا الخوف الله as much as you can.” النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) أيضاً said: “إذا I commanded you to do something, do whatever you can (of it).” Now it turns out that she can not stay at مكة المكرمة بينما she is unable لأن she harbors fears for herself, her religion and المال. She is not to be ordered to continue in her state of Ihram, return home and travel to مكة المكرمة again وإن كان she does not have the ability to do لذا and the same thing may happen again without the least الذنب on her part. Putting an end to the state of Ihram is not permissible and the obligatory act of circumambulation is still to be done. Circumambulation is almost similar to الصلاة. There is no proof that circumambulation is not accepted إذا performed by a person ina state of major ritual impurity. Yet, there are proofs that ritual purity is obligatory. ومن ثَم, there is disagreement among العلماء whether ritual purity is a condition or just an obligatory act. Such disagreement did not occur as far as الصلاة is concerned. The ثانياً proof is that ritual purity is a condition for circumambulation. It is well known that ritual purity is
